CUR.CRITICAL_NOTE: CUR committed 5 YC in 0-0 survival match vs ECU. Extreme foul rate in desperation.

market_calibrated.note: Calibrated to match Smarkets FTR: IVC=83.7%, Draw=11.1%, CUR=5.0%

context_flags: IVC_DRAW_ADVANCE: IVC only needs a draw → may park bus after scoring first goal
CUR_MUST_WIN: CUR extremely desperate (GD -6 makes 3rd place path impossible)
CUR_CARD_PATTERN: 5 YC vs ECU in GD2 (0-0 survival) → expect high cards in 2H desperation
CUR_SCORING: 1 goal in 2 WC2026 matches (consolation in 7-1 loss to GER)
IVC_DEFENSIVE_SHAPE: IVC likely plays MID-BLOCK (not high line) since draw = advance
CUR_OFFSIDE_LAMBDA: 1.5 avg offsides (1+2/2), but IVC mid-block reduces trap frequency

questions.text: Will Curaçao be caught offside 2 or more times?
questions.context: CUR must-win = aggressive forward runs = more offside risk. BUT IVC plays MID-BLOCK (draw = enough) not high line → fewer offside traps sprung. CUR's offside avg is 1.5 from GD1=1 and GD2=2. Moderate discount for IVC's defensive positioning.
questions.rule_applied: RULE_OFFSIDE_DEFENSIVE_SHAPE: IVC mid-block → discount from raw 44.2%

questions.text: Will Ivory Coast commit more fouls than Curaçao?
questions.context: CUR historically commits more fouls (10.5 vs 8.5). Must-win context if anything increases CUR's defensive fouling. IVC needing only a draw → cautious play → few attacking fouls. Strong model signal.

questions.text: Will Ivory Coast score the first goal of the second half?
questions.context: Market-calibrated IVC expected to score freely. P(IVC first 2H goal) = P(IVC ≥1 in 2H AND scores before CUR). If IVC leads at HT → may conserve energy → lower 2H rate. Conservative discount applied.

questions.text: Will the second half have more goals than the first half?

model.note: Equal per-half rates assumed. With λ_IVC_2H=1.323, λ_CUR_2H=0.233: P(2H > 1H) < 50%

questions.context: IVC likely to lead at HT (83.7% win prob) → may slow down in 2H to protect lead → fewer 2H goals. CUR's desperation might push 2H goals but unlikely to match a dominant IVC 1H tally. Under 50% likely.

questions.text: Will Curaçao have more shots on target than Ivory Coast in the second half?
questions.context: Raw model says 31.6% for CUR > IVC. CUR must-win in 2H (if trailing) = more aggressive SOT. IVC sitting on lead = fewer 2H SOT. Moderate upward adjustment for CUR's survival urgency. But IVC is still much higher quality.

questions.text: Will Curaçao receive more cards than Ivory Coast?
questions.context: STRONG SIGNAL: CUR committed 5 YC in GD2 vs ECU (desperate 0-0). Must-win survival match = maximum fouling from CUR. IVC only needs a draw → conservative play → fewer YC. Historical CUR avg 2.5 YC vs IVC 1.5 YC.

questions.text: Will Curaçao score at least 1 goal?
questions.market_anchor: BTTS YES = 40.3% → P(CUR ≥1 goal) ≈ BTTS + P(CUR wins without IVC scoring) ≈ 40%
questions.context: CUR scored 1 goal in 2 WC2026 matches (consolation in 7-1 loss). vs IVC defense (weaker than GER), CUR has slightly better chance. But market prices CUR win at only 5%. BTTS YES = 40.3% provides best anchor for CUR scoring.

questions.text: Will Ivory Coast have 5 or more corner kicks?
questions.data_note: NO ESPN corner data available for WC2026 matches (corners return '?' from API)
questions.context: IVC dominant (market 83.7% win, λ_IVC_goals=2.645). Many shots vs CUR who parks bus → corners. But IVC avg shots vs ECU/GER were moderate (15 and 9). Against weak CUR, likely higher. Estimate λ_corners ≈ 5 for IVC.
questions.confidence: low - no verified corner data, highly uncertain

questions.text: Will Leandro Bacuna have at least 1 shot on target?
questions.context: Market at 22.5%. CUR must-win = Bacuna more involved. vs IVC defense (weaker than GER). In GER-CUR GD1, we estimated 0.21 (market 0.32) → NO was correct. Now vs weaker IVC: slight upward vs GD1 estimate. Must-win urgency increases involvement slightly.
questions.prior_similar: GER-CUR GD1: Bacuna estimated 0.21 vs crowd 0.32 → NO → +9.76 RBP. Now vs weaker IVC.

questions.text: Will Ibrahim Sangaré have at least 1 shot on target?
questions.context: Sangaré is IVC's DM. In IVC's dominant performance, he may push forward. Market at 35.6%. IVC expected to dominate (λ=2.645 goals) → many attacking players shooting. As DM, Sangaré shoots less frequently but in a dominant win might get chances. Submit near market.

strongest_signals: Q2: IVC more fouls than CUR = 0.28 (NO - strong structural, CUR averages much higher fouls)
Q6: CUR more cards than IVC = 0.63 (YES - CUR 5YC in GD2, must-win desperation, strong pattern)
Q3: IVC first 2H goal = 0.65 (YES - dominant IVC, high market-calibrated goal rate)

Q1_CUR_offside_2plus.note: Small loss, near-push. RULE_OFFSIDE_DEFENSIVE_SHAPE discount (IVC mid-block) was directionally too aggressive — CUR's must-win desperation still generated enough forward runs to clear 2.

Q4_2H_goals_gt_1H.note: IVC, only needing a draw to advance, conserved energy in 2H exactly as modeled rather than piling on more goals.

Q5_CUR_more_2H_SOT.note: Crowd's lower 23% was closer to the truth — we over-credited CUR's survival urgency translating into 2H shot volume.

Q6_CUR_more_cards.note: The 5-YC desperation pattern from GD2 (0-0 vs ECU) repeated. Strong structural read on a team facing elimination.

Q8_IVC_5plus_corners.note: Biggest loss of the match — this was flagged as our lowest-confidence pick going in (no verified ESPN corner data, pure shots-to-corners heuristic at λ=5). The crowd's higher 64% had it right; IVC's corner generation against a parked-bus CUR exceeded our estimate.

Q9_Bacuna_SOT.note: Second consecutive correct NO call on Bacuna (after GER-CUR GD1's +9.76), now a confirmed 2/2 pattern for this player prop.

key_confirmations: Q8 is the clean lesson here: we explicitly flagged this as 'low confidence — no verified corner data' and it was the only meaningful loss. When ESPN doesn't have ground-truth for a stat, the shots-derived heuristic underestimates a dominant team's corner output against a deep-block opponent — lean the estimate UP, not toward 50/50, when confidence is genuinely low due to missing data (the uncertainty itself was correctly flagged, but the central estimate should have been adjusted toward the team-quality direction rather than parked at a toss-up).
Q9 (Bacuna) is now 2-for-2 across GER-CUR and CUR-IVC — worth tracking as a repeatable low-confidence player-prop NO.
